title: Values drop at end of scope
instruction:
When a variable goes out of scope, Rust drops its value and frees memory.

Run this.
successMessage: No free() call needed. Rust inserts it automatically at the closing brace. This is deterministic destruction.

title: Move semantics
instruction:
Assigning a `String` moves ownership. The original variable becomes invalid.

Run this — it will fail. Read the error.
hint: s1 was moved to s2. Use s2, or clone s1 before moving.

codeChecks.message: s1 was moved into s2. Use s2 instead, or clone s1 before the assignment.

successMessage: This prevents double-free. If both s1 and s2 tried to free the same memory, the program would crash. The move makes s1 invalid.

title: Clone
instruction:
`.clone()` makes a deep copy. Both variables own independent copies.

Run this.
successMessage: clone allocates new heap memory and copies the data. Both s1 and s2 are valid. Clone is intentionally verbose — heap allocation costs time.

title: Copy types
instruction:
Simple stack types like integers implement `Copy`. Assigning them copies the value — no move.

Run this.
successMessage: i32, f64, bool, char are all Copy. The stack copy is so cheap Rust does it automatically. Heap types like String are not Copy.

title: Passing to functions moves
instruction:
Passing a non-Copy value to a function moves it.

Run this.
successMessage: The function takes ownership of s. When consume returns, s is dropped. The caller can no longer use it.

title: Return gives back ownership
instruction:
A function can return a value to transfer ownership back to the caller.

Run this.
successMessage: The function creates a String and returns it — ownership goes to g. When main ends, g is dropped.

title: Stack vs heap
instruction:
Stack values (integers, booleans, arrays of fixed size) implement Copy.
Heap values (String, Vec) are moved.

Run this.
successMessage: Fixed-size arrays are Copy (stack-allocated). Vec is not Copy (heap-allocated). The pattern: if the size is known at compile time, it is usually Copy.

title: Ownership summary
instruction:
Three rules:
1. Each value has exactly one owner
2. There can only be one owner at a time
3. When the owner goes out of scope, the value is dropped

Run this to confirm.
successMessage: Three rules, zero garbage collector. The compiler enforces all of this at compile time. No runtime overhead.
